title: Pyrus zayulensis
taxonomy: Pyrus zayulensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. It belongs to the genus Pyrus, which includes various species of pear trees. Pyrus zayulensis is a part of the order Rosales and is further classified under the subclass Rosidae. This classification places Pyrus zayulensis within the larger group of flowering plants known as angiosperms. The exact classification of Pyrus zayulensis is still evolving, but it is generally recognized as a member of the family Rosaceae.

The leaves and bark of Pyrus zayulensis are traditionally used in traditional medicine, particularly in the treatment of respiratory issues and fever. intro:
There is limited information available on the plant Pyrus zayulensis. However, based on available information, Pyrus zayulensis is a species of pear tree. 

Pyrus zayulensis is said to have various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. Its bioactive constituents include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids.
